subject: Public Health
title: Nicotine: The Hook
description: A short lesson to help you apply Nicotine: The Hook.

hook.text: Nicotine reaches the brain in 10 seconds—faster than any injected drug—creating one of the strongest addictions known.

buildup.text: Nicotine triggers dopamine release, producing pleasure and calm—then withdrawal hits, driving the next cigarette.

discovery.text: Tobacco companies engineered cigarettes to deliver nicotine more efficiently—adding ammonia to increase absorption.

twist.text: Nicotine itself isn't the main killer—it's the 7,000 other chemicals in cigarette smoke that cause cancer and disease.

climax.text: Nicotine replacement therapy—patches, gums, and lozenges—delivers nicotine safely and doubles quit success rates.

punchline.text: Nicotine is the hook. Smoke is the killer. Separating them saves lives.

quiz.question: How quickly does nicotine reach the brain after inhalation?
